title: Dual stack pod no longer accessible after a restart
body:
### Issue Description

Using an ipv6 enabled network with a pod with an nginx container only works on boot. As soon as the pod is restarted, the nginx server is no longer reachable.

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Use the following quadlets:

```
# cat /etc/containers/systemd/podman-dual-stack.network 
[Unit]
Description=Dual stack network
[Network]
IPv6=true
```

```
# cat /etc/containers/systemd/dual-stack.pod 
[Unit]
Description=Dual stack pod
[Pod]
Network=podman-dual-stack.network
PublishPort=0.0.0.0:80:80
PublishPort=[::]:80:80
```

```
# cat /etc/containers/systemd/nginx-dual-stack.container 
[Unit]
Description=nginx (dual stack)
[Container]
Pod=dual-stack.pod
Image=docker.io/nginx:latest
[Install]
WantedBy=default.target
```

This configuration will give a working setup on boot:

```
# curl -4 localhost:80
...
```

However, as soon as the pod is restarted, the nginx server is no longer reachable:

```
# systemctl restart dual-stack-pod.service 
# curl -4 localhost:80
curl: (7) Failed to connect to localhost port 80 after 3108 ms: Could not connect to server
```

The nginx server is properly listening:
```
# podman exec -ti systemd-nginx-dual-stack curl localhost
...
```

And we can see conmon listening on the ports:

```
# sudo ss -latpn
State    Recv-Q   Send-Q                Local Address:Port       Peer Address:Port    Process                                                                  
...
LISTEN   0        4096                        0.0.0.0:80              0.0.0.0:*        users:(("conmon",pid=3537,fd=5))                                        
...                     
LISTEN   0        4096                           [::]:80                 [::]:*        users:(("conmon",pid=3537,fd=6))                                        
...
```

### Describe the results you received

Container no longer reachable after a pod restart

### Describe the results you expected

Container reachable after a pod restart

### podman info output


### Additional information

comments.body:
This is a dup of https://github.com/containers/netavark/issues/1338

In short do not use both
```
PublishPort=0.0.0.0:80:80
PublishPort=[::]:80:80
```

at the moment, just 
```
PublishPort=80:80
```
should be enough to forward both v6 and v4
Thanks, this works indeed. The output of `ss` is confusing here.

> The output of ss is confusing here.

as root the bind are meaningless for the most part expect conflicts check as the firewall handles the traffic. That said there is a fix in progress that should fix that in 6.0 https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/27311
